plains-of-moab.name: Plains of Moab
plains-of-moab.modernName: Jordan Valley, east of Jordan River near Dead Sea
plains-of-moab.description: The plains east of the Jordan River opposite Jericho, where Moses addressed Israel before entering Canaan.

mount-sinai.name: Mount Sinai
mount-sinai.description: The mountain where God gave the Law to Moses. Also called Horeb in Deuteronomy.

mount-seir.name: Mount Seir
mount-seir.description: Mountain range in the land of Edom, territory of the descendants of Esau.

kadesh-barnea.name: Kadesh-barnea
kadesh-barnea.description: An oasis settlement where Israel encamped and from which the twelve spies were sent into Canaan.

bashan-ashtaroth.name: Ashtaroth (Bashan)
bashan-ashtaroth.description: A city in Bashan, one of the royal cities of Og king of Bashan.

arabah.name: Arabah
arabah.description: The great rift valley extending from the Sea of Galilee to the Gulf of Aqaba.

hill-country-canaan.name: Hill Country of Canaan
hill-country-canaan.description: The mountainous interior of Canaan, inhabited by the Amorites, which Israel was commanded to conquer.

negev.name: Negev
negev.description: The arid region in the south of Canaan, part of the land promised to Israel.

coastal-plain.name: Coastal Plain
coastal-plain.description: The low-lying region along the Mediterranean coast, part of the promised land described in Deuteronomy 1.

lebanon.name: Lebanon
lebanon.description: The northern mountain range marking the extent of the promised land.

euphrates-river.name: Euphrates River
euphrates-river.description: The great river forming the northeastern boundary of the promised land as described by God to Moses.

land-of-canaan.name: Land of Canaan
land-of-canaan.description: The land promised by God to the descendants of Abraham, which Israel was preparing to enter.

wilderness-of-paran.name: Wilderness of Paran
wilderness-of-paran.description: A large desert region in the Sinai Peninsula through which Israel traveled from Sinai to Kadesh-barnea.

valley-of-eshcol.name: Valley of Eshcol
valley-of-eshcol.description: The valley near Hebron where the spies cut a cluster of grapes as evidence of the land's fertility.

hill-country-kadesh.name: Hill Country near Kadesh
altNames: Hill Country of the Amorites near Kadesh
hill-country-kadesh.modernName: Hill region near Kadesh-barnea, northeastern Sinai
hill-country-kadesh.description: The hilly terrain near Kadesh-barnea where the Israelites were defeated after disobeying God's command.

hormah.name: Hormah
hormah.description: A settlement in the Negev where the Israelites were pursued and defeated by the Amorites after their disobedient attempt to enter Canaan.

seir-edom.name: Seir (Edom)
seir-edom.description: The region south of the Dead Sea belonging to the descendants of Esau, through which Israel traveled.

edrei.name: Edrei
edrei.description: A city in Bashan where Israel defeated Og, king of Bashan, as referenced in the introduction to Deuteronomy.

hill-country-hebron.name: Hill Country near Hebron
hill-country-hebron.description: The hill country near Hebron where the spies explored the land of Canaan.

wilderness-red-sea.name: Wilderness toward the Red Sea
wilderness-red-sea.description: The wilderness route leading toward the Red Sea (Gulf of Aqaba), where Israel wandered after the rebellion at Kadesh-barnea.

amorite-territory.name: Amorite Territory
amorite-territory.description: The hill country inhabited by the Amorites, where Israel was defeated after disobeying God at Kadesh-barnea.
